text.title: In-Issue Ad Format Library Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in newsletter in-issue ad rotations. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix keyed to a methodology rule.

antipattern.symptom: CTR on top_banner drops below the baseline for 3 consecutive issues; unsubscribe rate spikes.
antipattern.root-cause: top_banner used every issue without rotation; readers stop noticing.
antipattern.fix: Enforce r2 cap of 1×/issue but rotate at least 1-in-3 issues to soft_ps or none. Validator rejects rotations that load top_banner past the 6-issue ceiling.

antipattern.symptom: Unsubscribe rate doubles after the 2nd dedicated_send in a 6-issue window; founder pushes a third anyway.
antipattern.root-cause: dedicated_send treated as default for product launches; cap ignored.
antipattern.fix: Enforce r2 cap of 1×/6 issues. Validator rejects plans with 2+ dedicated_send in any 6-issue window. Use feature_announcement CTA in soft_ps as the secondary channel.

antipattern.symptom: Founder claims "format X is working" but no baseline figure exists; format selection drifts with vibes.
antipattern.root-cause: No 90-day CTR/CVR calc; format reviews are anecdotal.
antipattern.fix: Compute baseline per r3 before adding rotation plan. Validator requires baseline.computed_at populated and within 30 days.

antipattern.symptom: CTR looks normal but CVR collapses — clicks land on a page that offers both a lead magnet and a paywall purchase.
antipattern.root-cause: cta_destination field set to mixed; one button serves two goals.
antipattern.fix: Single destination per ad per r5. Validator rejects rotation_plan rows with cta_destination='mixed'.
